moduleDescription: Some type definitions for compatibility between different backends and platforms.

entries.name: BiggestInt
entries.description: is an alias for the biggest signed integer type the Nim compiler supports. Currently this is int64, but it is platform-dependent in general.

entries.name: BiggestFloat
entries.description: is an alias for the biggest floating point type the Nim compiler supports. Currently this is float64, but it is platform-dependent in general.

entries.name: BiggestUInt
entries.description: is an alias for the biggest unsigned integer type the Nim compiler supports. Currently this is uint64, but it is platform-dependent in general.

entries.name: clong
entries.description:
Represents the C long type, used for interoperability.

Its purpose is to match the C long for the target platform's Application Binary Interface (ABI).

Typically, the compiler resolves it to one of the following Nim types based on the target:

  • int32 on Windows using MSVC or MinGW compilers.
  • int on Linux, macOS and other platforms that use the LP64 or ILP32 data models.
Warning: The underlying Nim type is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon.

entries.name: culong
entries.description:
Represents the C unsigned long type, used for interoperability.

Its purpose is to match the C unsigned long for the target platform's Application Binary Interface (ABI).

Typically, the compiler resolves it to one of the following Nim types based on the target:

  • uint32 on Windows using MSVC or MinGW compilers.
  • uint on Linux, macOS and other platforms that use the LP64 or ILP32 data models.
Warning: The underlying Nim type is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon.

entries.name: cchar
entries.description: This is the same as the type char in C.

entries.name: cschar
entries.description: This is the same as the type signed char in C.

entries.name: cshort
entries.description: This is the same as the type short in C.

entries.name: cint
entries.description: This is the same as the type int in C.

entries.name: csize_t
entries.description: This is the same as the type size_t in C.

entries.name: clonglong
entries.description: This is the same as the type long long in C.

entries.name: cfloat
entries.description: This is the same as the type float in C.

entries.name: cdouble
entries.description: This is the same as the type double in C.

entries.name: clongdouble
entries.description: This is the same as the type long double in C. This C type is not supported by Nim's code generator.

entries.name: cuchar
entries.code:
cuchar {.importc: "unsigned char", nodecl,
         deprecated: "Use `char` or `uint8` instead".} = char

entries.name: cushort
entries.description: This is the same as the type unsigned short in C.

entries.name: cuint
entries.description: This is the same as the type unsigned int in C.

entries.name: culonglong
entries.description: This is the same as the type unsigned long long in C.

entries.name: ByteAddress
entries.description: is the signed integer type that should be used for converting pointers to integer addresses for readability.

entries.name: cstringArray
entries.description: This is binary compatible to the type char** in C. The array's high value is large enough to disable bounds checking in practice. Use cstringArrayToSeq proc to convert it into a seq[string].

entries.name: PFloat32
entries.description: An alias for ptr float32.

entries.name: PFloat64
entries.description: An alias for ptr float64.

entries.name: PInt64
entries.description: An alias for ptr int64.

entries.name: PInt32
entries.description: An alias for ptr int32.
